mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations during payroll expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market slack, reducing household income growth and triggering downward earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return during trigger periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and declines in industrial production indicate restrictive monetary policy propagating into real supply contraction, jointly amplifying downside risk to corporate profits and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS changes and INDPRO changes yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the simple FEDFUNDS-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in real GDP growth exceeding one percentage point annualized quarter-over-quarter signals demand saturation and looming earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns follow fewer than 50% of deceleration triggers in late-cycle regimes, or the strategy Sharpe ratio in these regimes is below 0.3 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals accelerating residential investment and upstream demand for construction materials, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances during early-cycle regimes, or the mean 5-day return conditional on the trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stagnant retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and supply-demand imbalance, compressing corporate margins and forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO up and RSAFS flat or down produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index signals mounting inflation pressure that raises discount rates and erodes real corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of CPIAUCSL changes falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of high-inflation-acceleration months.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate greater than 25 basis points over three months confirms a shift to monetary tightening, raising borrowing costs and reducing equity risk appetite, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns follow fewer than 55% of tightening triggers, or the mean return during active tightening episodes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
